task.name: AR Glasses Transparent Materials Discovery
task.objective: Discover lightweight transparent conductive, dielectric, and barrier materials for all-day wearable AR glasses.

candidate.name: Magnesium aluminate spinel
candidate.rationale: Transparent ceramic spinel with useful hardness and optical-window heritage. Retrieval context: transparent_conductor, oxide, barrier.

synthesis.route: precursor mixing, calcination, densification or film deposition, annealing, and characterization

mechanism_hypotheses: dense oxygen framework can combine optical transmission with hardness and thermal stability
wide-gap chemistry supports visible transparency for optical stacks
tradeoff_notes: no high-severity chemistry tradeoff triggered by current heuristics
validation_priorities: DFT relaxation, band structure, and defect energetics
thin-film optical constants, haze, stress, and humidity aging

next_experiments: Run DFT bandgap and defect screening for MgAl2O4.
Simulate thin-film optical stack impact at target wavelength bands.
Measure film stress, haze, adhesion, and accelerated humidity stability.
